topic: Create and configure Azure Backup policies
key_facts: Azure Backup now supports immutable vaults that help you ensure that after recovery points are created, they can't be deleted before their expiry according to the backup policy.
When you configure backup, you can browse to the VM name and its resource group, even though you don't have the required permission on those VMs.
This phase ensures a recovery point is available for Instant Restore for up to 30 days, depending on the configured policy type and snapshot retention.

To create a Recovery Services vault:

 
 Sign in to the Azure portal .

On the Create Recovery Services vault pane, enter the following values:

 
 Subscription : Select the subscription to use.
If you're a member of only one subscription, you see that name.
If you're not sure which subscription to use, use the default subscription.
